connection_endpoint.h(3) Library Functions Manual connection_endpoint.h(3)

NAME

connection_endpoint.h - Shared endpoint normalization for TCP and WebSocket connections.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct connection_endpoint_t

Enumerations


enum connection_endpoint_protocol_t { CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP = 0, CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET = 1 }

Functions


asciichat_error_t connection_endpoint_resolve (const char *input, uint16_t default_port, connection_endpoint_t *endpoint_out)
const char * connection_endpoint_protocol_name (connection_endpoint_protocol_t protocol)

Detailed Description

Shared endpoint normalization for TCP and WebSocket connections.

This helper removes repeated URL/scheme parsing across client and discovery connection setup code. Callers get one normalized endpoint description and can then choose the appropriate transport path.

Definition in file connection_endpoint.h.

CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET

  connection_endpoint.c.


12                                                                                        {
13   switch (protocol) {
14   case CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP:
15     return "TCP";
16   case CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET:
17     return "WebSocket";
18   default:

References CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP, and
    CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET.


Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp().






asciichat_error_t connection_endpoint_resolve (const char
  * input, uint16_t default_port, connection_endpoint_t *
  endpoint_out)


Resolve an input string into a normalized TCP or WebSocket
    endpoint.




Supported inputs:


    
  
  • ws://host[:port]
    • 
  
  • wss://host[:port]
    • 
  
  • tcp://host[:port]
    • 
  
  • bare hostnames or host:port pairs
    • 



Bare hostnames are treated as TCP endpoints and use default_port
    when the input does not specify a port.


Definition at line 39 of file
  connection_endpoint.c.


40                                                                                    {
41   if (!input || !*input || !endpoint_out) {
42     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "input or endpoint_out is NULL");
43   }
44 
45   url_parts_t parts = {0};
46   asciichat_error_t result;
47 
48   if (url_is_websocket(input)) {
49     result = url_parse(input, &parts);
50     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
51       return result;
52     }
53     result = connection_endpoint_from_parts(endpoint_out, &parts, CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET, default_port, input);
54     url_parts_destroy(&parts);
55     return result;
56   }
57 
58   if (url_is_tcp(input)) {
59     result = url_parse(input, &parts);
60     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
61       return result;
62     }
63     result = connection_endpoint_from_parts(endpoint_out, &parts, CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP, default_port, input);
64     url_parts_destroy(&parts);
65     return result;
66   }
67 
68   /* Unsupported explicit scheme. Bare hostnames are allowed and have no "://". */
69   if (strstr(input, "://") != NULL) {
70     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Unsupported connection scheme: %s", input);
71   }
72 
73   result = url_parse(input, &parts);
74   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
75     return result;
76   }
77 
78   result = connection_endpoint_from_parts(endpoint_out, &parts, CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP, default_port, input);
79   url_parts_destroy(&parts);
80   return result;
81 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_TCP,
    CONNECTION_ENDPOINT_WEBSOCKET, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    SET_ERRNO, url_is_tcp(), url_is_websocket(),
    url_parse(), and url_parts_destroy().


Referenced by connection_attempt_tcp(),
    connection_factory_open(), and
  server_connection_establish().
